items.notes: Hydrogen PEM fuel cell modules for UAVs (IE-SOAR line) plus automotive, aviation, MHE, and stationary power applications. All products are power generation/supply systems.

products_categories.notes: All product lines (IE-SOAR, IE-LIFT, IE-POWER, IE-DRIVE, IE-FLIGHT, IE-GRID) are hydrogen fuel cell power systems for various sectors.

items.name: IE-SOAR 800
items.product_type: 800W hydrogen fuel cell module for UAVs

evidence.snippet: **IE-SOAR™ 800** is Intelligent Energy’s fuel cell module optimised for VTOL and fixed wing [applications](https://www.intelligent-energy.com/our-industries/uav/). Using our lightweight fuel cell technology and a modular structure, our 800W module offers greater flexibility to the end user and extends time in the air.

evidence.snippet: **IE-FLIGHT**™ is our high-power product line for the aviation sector. From 200kW to multi-megawatt scale, these systems have been developed to achieve zero-emission flight for eVTOL, Part-23 and Part-25 aircraft serving sub-regional and regional markets.

evidence.snippet: **IE-SOAR™ 800** is Intelligent Energy’s fuel cell module optimised for VTOL and fixed wing [applications](https://www.intelligent-energy.com/our-industries/uav/). Using our lightweight fuel cell technology and a modular structure, our 800W module offers greater flexibility to the end user and extends time in the air.

readiness.notes: IE-SOAR 800 carries a 1000-hour commercial warranty with published datasheets and commercial case studies (Cyberhawk, Shell, Aurora). IE-FLIGHT is still in government-funded R&D phase; secondary mode is prototype/engineering_services for that product line.

tagline: UK-based hydrogen fuel cell manufacturer producing modular PEM systems across UAV, stationary power, material handling, and automotive applications. Core UAV offerings—IE-SOAR family (800W–2.4kW)—are in production for multi-rotor, fixed-wing, and VTOL platforms. Broader portfolio includes IE-FLIGHT (200kW–multi-MW for eVTOL), IE-POWER stationary modules, IE-DRIVE automotive systems, and IE-GRID containerized backup power. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown; no major US defense contracts identified in public record. Primary addressable market appears to be allied defense and critical infrastructure; supply-chain fit for US primes requires clarification on export control, IP location, and production capacity for defense-rate volumes.
